task_updates.executor_notes: Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `TrackLabel` now disables the up/down controls using `kindIndex` and `sameKindCount`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` from preceding same-kind tracks. This fixes the mixed-kind boundary case where the first audio track was incorrectly treated as movable upward based on global index.

Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `handleReorderTrack` now scans in the requested direction until it finds the next track of the same kind before swapping. Mixed-kind boundaries now no-op when no same-kind target exists, while same-kind neighbors still reorder normally.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `TrackLabel` now checks `kindIndex === 0`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` by counting preceding same-kind tracks. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, `A1` has global index 2 but `kindIndex` 0, so the up button disables correctly.
Yes. The reorder handler now scans for the next same-kind track before swapping. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, moving `V2` down finds no later visual track and no-ops; moving `A1` down finds `A2` and swaps.
